prevalence.value: 15-30% of hospitalized older adults; higher in ICU

vulnerablePopulations: older adults with baseline cognitive impairment
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis with CAM/4AT screening when used; identify reversible causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; limited staffing affects prevention bundle implementation

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often missed, especially hypoactive delirium; screening tools inconsistently applied
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Treat underlying cause, hydration, mobilization, sleep protocols; minimize sedatives; antipsychotics only for severe agitation when necessary
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: High inpatient volumes and limited geriatric services
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Low-cost prevention strategies can reduce delirium when implemented
